tasks.description: Implement `src/shared/dev/useRenderBudget.ts` with dev-gated runtime behavior, per-mount render counting, 1s idle reset, first-over-budget warning per active window, and an in-file telemetry registry/subscription API aggregated by component name using `max` active count (status red when `maxCount > budget`). While doing this, confirm target component names/paths and wrapper insertion points (`TasksPaneComponent`, `PreviewPanelComponent`) and lock timeline row target to `TrackListRenderer.tsx` `SortableRow`; halt and remap immediately if any named target is missing.

Add `src/shared/dev/RenderTelemetryOverlay.tsx` as a fixed bottom-right dev widget using Tailwind theme tokens (no hardcoded colors), hidden by default with localStorage persistence, live subscription to telemetry, and a non-conflicting global toggle shortcut (explicitly avoid `Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+R`); mount it from `src/app/App.tsx` only in dev.

Instrument required surfaces by calling `useRenderBudget` at top of executable render functions with initial budgets: `ImageLightbox`(5), `EditModePanel`(3), `LightboxShell`(5), `TimelineCanvas`(3), `TrackListRenderer` `SortableRow`(2), `MediaGalleryItem`(3), `TasksPaneComponent` name `TasksPane`(5), `ShotsPanelContent`(5), `GenerationsPaneGallery`(5), `PreviewPanelComponent` name `PreviewPanel`(5). Keep changes insertion-only (no render/state refactors).

Capture baseline metrics on a non-HMR development-mode built artifact (`build:dev` + preview or equivalent), run normal interaction flows, and calibrate budgets upward to observed max + 1 where needed so baseline behavior is not perpetually warning. Collect at least 5 components × 3 interactions each, including mount plus applicable flows (lightbox open, clip select, pane toggle).

Create `docs/state_management_refactor_baselines.md` with capture date and commit SHA, per-component baseline table, interaction-sequence sections, and observations. Explicitly document aggregation semantics (`max` active per-mount count per component name in 1s window) to lock future comparability.

Run scope/compliance pass: ensure edits are limited to approved locations (`src/shared/dev/`, `src/app/App.tsx`, `docs/`, and explicitly instrumented files), confirm existing diagnostics remain additive (not removed/refactored), and verify no prohibited subsystems were touched.

Final verification and bug-repro smoke: run `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm test`, and `npm run build`; grep runtime `dist` assets excluding sourcemaps for `RenderBudget` and `RenderTelemetryOverlay`; verify dev toggle/persistence/live updates and synthetic over-budget warning path. Also create a short throwaway script that reproduces the targeted M0 telemetry behavior (over-budget warning/reset and/or production-string stripping check), run it, confirm outcome, then delete the script. If any check fails, fix and re-run until green.

watch_items: Aggregation contract is fixed: overlay/docs use per-name `max` active count in the 1s window; do not switch to sum/latest.
Telemetry and overlay must remain dev-only at runtime; production bundles must not contain `RenderBudget` or `RenderTelemetryOverlay` in JS/CSS assets (ignore `.map`).
Avoid keybind collision with existing AgentChat `Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+R` binding; choose and document a conflict-free shortcut.
Use hook placement in executable inner components (`TasksPaneComponent`, `PreviewPanelComponent`) rather than memoized wrappers.
Timeline row instrumentation is specifically `TrackListRenderer.tsx` `SortableRow`; do not leave this abstract.
Baseline capture must be from non-HMR dev-mode built artifact, not production mode and not HMR session.
Budgets are heuristic starts; raise to observed max + 1 to avoid noisy false alarms during normal baseline interactions.
Overlay styling must use Tailwind theme tokens only; no hardcoded color literals.
Keep existing render diagnostics (`debugRendering`, `usePerfDiagnostics`) untouched; new telemetry is additive.
Do not expand scope into render optimization/refactors, stores (Zustand), or non-approved directories.

sense_checks.question: Does `useRenderBudget` compile with hooks declared unconditionally, runtime dev-gating in place, 1s idle reset + warning behavior implemented, and registry aggregation/status defined as `max` per component name?
Is overlay mounted only in dev, hidden by default with persisted visibility, visually tokenized via Tailwind theme classes, and toggled by a shortcut that does not conflict with existing bindings?
Are all required component surfaces instrumented at the correct inner render function with the intended names/budgets, including `SortableRow` in `TrackListRenderer.tsx`?
Were baseline counts captured from a non-HMR dev build/preview across at least 5 components with at least 3 interactions each, and were over-noisy budgets raised to observed max + 1?
Does `state_management_refactor_baselines.md` include date, commit SHA, per-component table, interaction narratives, and explicit `max` aggregation semantics for comparability?
Are changes confined to approved files/paths with no forbidden subsystem edits and no refactoring beyond instrumentation/overlay/hook/doc scope?

Do `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` pass; are runtime dist assets clean of target strings (excluding `.map`); did dev smoke + synthetic over-budget path pass; and was the throwaway repro script removed after execution?

meta_commentary: Execute in plan order, but treat baseline capture as the truth source that can force budget bumps and minor naming clarifications before final verification. Preserve strict M0 scope: instrumentation and measurement only. If any named component/file differs from the brief, resolve to the exact in-repo equivalent and document it in the baseline notes rather than improvising new surfaces. Keep the aggregation operator immutable (`max`) across hook, overlay, and docs to avoid invalidating M1+ comparisons.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Reconfirm scope, target files, timeline row surface, wrapper insertion points, and additive coexistence
Step 2: Implement dev-only render-budget hook and shared registry with deterministic aggregation
Step 3: Add dev-only telemetry overlay, persistence, non-conflicting toggle, root mount
Step 4: Instrument all required component surfaces with initial budgets
Step 5: Calibrate budgets on dev-mode built artifact and capture/document baselines
Step 6: Run typecheck/tests/build, production stripping grep, and dev smoke validation

validation.completeness_notes: All six approved plan steps are mapped. Execution-order and validation-order intents are preserved via task dependencies and the final verification task. Accepted tradeoff on aggregation is explicitly locked as `max` in implementation and documentation checkpoints.

baseline_test_note: No supported test runner detected on PATH (looked for: pytest). Configure test_command in state config to specify one.
